summary:
Suctioning should last no more than 10-15 seconds per attempt due to potential risks associated with longer procedures; this minimizes damage while preserving airway health.
To address your request accurately within two concise sentences focusing only on critical aspects related directly to when tracheal secretions need to be suctioned based upon typical medical practice standards:

Suction is indicated if it improves oxygenation or ventilation but generally limited by time constraints such as not exceeding about 10-15 seconds each pass to avoid harm from excessive manipulation. The primary goal remains enhancing respiratory function safely under close monitoring conditions appropriate for intubated patients requiring mechanical support. 

This summary captures essential elements around timing limits along with rationale behind them pertinent specifically towards addressing initial query posed above concerning indications & practical application limitations surrounding tracheal suction care practices.
